description:
You are a machine-learning engineer doing quick, command-line data preparation.
    A raw text file that simulates casual chat messages is already present on the
    system at

        /home/user/datasets/chat_samples.txt

    1. Convert the contents of **chat_samples.txt** to a case-insensitive, 
       punctuation-free word list.  Contractions are not present, so a simple
       character-class-based strip is sufficient; only the twenty-six letters
       <a–z>, the digits <0–9> and ASCII white-space should remain.  All letters
       must be forced to lowercase before counting.

    2. Calculate the frequency of every distinct token
       (the definition of “token” here is “a contiguous sequence of the
       characters that remain after the cleaning step”).
       Do not remove any so-called *stop words*; every term must be counted.

    3. Write the final frequency table to the file

        /home/user/datasets/chat_samples_freq.tsv

       in the exact, two-column, TAB-separated format shown below:

            word<TAB>count<NEWLINE>

       • Column 1: the token (all lowercase, no surrounding spaces).  
       • Column 2: its integer frequency (base-10, no leading zeros).  
       • Each record ends with a single UNIX newline (LF, 0x0A).  
       • There must be **no header row** and **no extra blank lines**.

    4. Sorting requirements for **chat_samples_freq.tsv** that the automated
       grader will enforce:

       a. Primary key: descending numerical frequency  
       b. Secondary key: ascending ASCII/lexicographic order of the tokens  
       c. 100 % deterministic – identical runs produce byte-identical files

       As a consequence, the very first three lines of the file must all have
       the same frequency value (5) and appear in the alphabetic order
       “ai”, “hello”, “world”.

    5. Create a simple, plain-text command log called

        /home/user/datasets/build_freq_commands.log

       • Append every shell command you actually executed *exactly as typed*,
         one per line, in the order they were run.  
       • This log must contain **at least five (5) distinct commands** and
         reflect reasonable use of standard GNU utilities (e.g., `tr`, `sed`,
         `awk`, `sort`, `uniq`, etc.).  
       • No output or commentary lines – only the commands themselves.

    6. When you are done, you should have created exactly the two new files
       specified above, with read-write permissions for the current user.
       Nothing else on the filesystem should be modified.

    The automated tests will verify:

       • The existence and permissions of the two new files.  
       • The exact byte-for-byte contents and ordering of
         /home/user/datasets/chat_samples_freq.tsv.  
       • That the log file has ≥ 5 non-empty lines, each containing a plausible
         shell command.

    Reminder: you do *not* have root privileges.  Work entirely under
    /home/user.  You may inspect the raw dataset with any tools you like,
    but only the above two deliverables will be graded.
truth:
Initial files present before the task starts
    ===========================================

    1. /home/user/datasets/chat_samples.txt  (permissions 0644)

       Exact eight-line content, including punctuation and trailing newlines:

       ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
       Hello world, hello AI!
       The AI world is vast.
       Machine learning fuels the AI revolution.
       Hello again, machine learning world.
       AI and machine learning are related fields.
       World of hello messages.
       AI says hello to the world.
       Learning about machine intelligence.
       ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


    Expected artefacts after successful completion
    ==============================================

    1. /home/user/datasets/chat_samples_freq.tsv  (permissions 0644)

       Exact required content (21 lines, each LF-terminated):

       ai	5
       hello	5
       world	5
       learning	4
       machine	4
       the	3
       about	1
       again	1
       and	1
       are	1
       fields	1
       fuels	1
       intelligence	1
       is	1
       messages	1
       of	1
       related	1
       revolution	1
       says	1
       to	1
       vast	1

    2. /home/user/datasets/build_freq_commands.log  (permissions 0644)

       • Must exist.  
       • Must contain ≥ 5 non-empty lines.  
       • Each line must correspond to a shell command executed during the task.
       • No predetermined content – the test harness will only count lines
         and ensure they are not blank/whitespace-only.

    No other files or directories should be created or altered.
